lessons.title: Greetings, names & the alphabet
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Hello, hi, goodbye, bye, good morning/afternoon/evening/night. My name is… / I'm… Nice to meet you. The English alphabet — spelling names aloud. Numbers 1–10."

lessons.title: To be — I am, you are (positive & negative)
grammar: To be — I am, you are
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "I am / I'm · You are / You're · I am not / You are not. Questions: Are you…? Yes, I am / No, I'm not."

lessons.title: Meeting someone for the first time
lessons.recycled: Greetings vocabulary + I am / I'm + Are you…?
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Students introduce themselves: name, spelling (alphabet), and a simple greeting exchange."

lessons.title: Spoken course lesson: Hello, who are you?
lessons.recycled: Full active revision of Unit 1 vocabulary and grammar in free conversation.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Pure conversation around self-introduction with no written support."

lessons.title: Unit 1 exam
lessons.recycled: All Unit 1 content tested actively.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Listening: identify greetings. Reading: match names to spellings. Writing: complete introduction card. Speaking: introduce yourself."

units.title: Where are you from? — Countries & nationalities

lessons.title: Countries, nationalities & languages
lessons.recycled: Numbers 1–10 from Unit 1 extended to 1–20.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Countries, nationalities & languages. Numbers 11–20."

lessons.title: To be — he/she/it is · They are · Questions
lessons.recycled: I am / You are from Unit 1.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "He is / She is / It is + contractions. They are. Where is he from? Where are you from?"

lessons.title: Talking about where people are from
lessons.recycled: ↻ Greetings (Unit 1), I am / You are (Unit 1), new he/she forms, Numbers.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Students describe photos of famous people: She is Italian. He is from Japan."

lessons.title: Spoken course lesson: Where are you from?
lessons.recycled: Unit 1 greetings and self-introduction woven into the opening. All to be forms active.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Students ask and answer freely about origins, languages spoken, nationalities."

lessons.title: Unit 2 exam
lessons.recycled: Unit 1 alphabet, greetings, and I am integrated.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Listening: identify nationalities. Reading: true/false person's profile. Writing: write 3 sentences about a person."

lessons.title: Family members & ages
lessons.recycled: Numbers 1–20 from Units 1–2 extended to 100.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Family members & ages. Numbers 20–100 for ages. Old/young, tall/short."

lessons.title: Possessive adjectives — my, your, his, her, our, their
lessons.recycled: All to be forms (Units 1–2) appear in model sentences.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "My mother, your brother, his sister, her husband, our family, their children. Introducing 's for possession."

lessons.title: Talking about your family
lessons.recycled: ↻ To be (Units 1–2), Nationalities/countries (Unit 2), Alphabet, Numbers 1–100.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Students describe their real or an imaginary family: ages, names, nationalities, relationships."

lessons.title: Spoken course lesson: Tell me about your family
lessons.recycled: Full active revision of Units 1–3: greetings, to be, nationalities, possessives, ages.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Free conversation about family."

lessons.title: Unit 3 exam
lessons.recycled: Units 1–3 integrated: greetings, to be, nationalities, possessives, numbers.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Listening: identify family members. Reading: fill family tree. Writing: short paragraph about family."

lessons.title: Rooms, furniture & prepositions of place
lessons.recycled: Big/small adjectives from Unit 3. Numbers for room count.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Rooms, furniture & prepositions: in, on, under, next to, in front of, behind, between."

lessons.title: There is / there are — positive, negative, questions
lessons.recycled: Numbers (Units 1–3) for counting furniture. To be (Units 1–2). Adjectives (Unit 3).
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "There is a table. There are two chairs. Is there a garden? Yes, there is. / No, there isn't."

lessons.title: Describing your home
lessons.recycled: ↻ My/your/his/her (Unit 3), To be (Units 1–2), Nationalities, Numbers.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Students describe their home room by room: where things are, what there is, how many rooms."

lessons.title: Spoken course lesson: Your perfect home
lessons.recycled: Units 1–4 fully active: to be, possessives, numbers, nationalities, there is/are.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Students describe their ideal home."

lessons.title: Unit 4 exam
lessons.recycled: Units 1–4: full integration. Family + home = natural overlap.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Listening: identify rooms. Reading: read flat description. Writing: describe living room."

lessons.title: Daily routine verbs & time expressions
lessons.recycled: Good morning/evening from Unit 1.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Routine verbs. Time: o'clock, half past, quarter past/to. In the morning/afternoon/evening."

lessons.title: Present simple — I / you / we / they (affirmative, negative, questions)
lessons.recycled: To be (Units 1–2) contrasted with present simple for habits. Time vocabulary.
grammar: Frequency adverbs: always, usually, sometimes, never
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "I wake up at 7. Do you go to work? Yes, I do. / No, I don't. Adverb position."

lessons.title: Talking about your daily routine
lessons.recycled: ↻ Family vocab (Unit 3), Possessives (Unit 3), Time + numbers (Units 1–4), there is (Unit 4).
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Students describe a full weekday."

lessons.title: Spoken course lesson: A day in my life
lessons.recycled: Units 1–5 all active. Greetings open and close naturally.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Students narrate their full day spontaneously."

lessons.title: Unit 5 exam
lessons.recycled: Units 1–5 integrated. Writing and speaking demand simultaneous use of to be, possessives, and present simple.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Listening: order routine. Reading: routine comprehension. Writing: typical Tuesday profile."

lessons.title: Food, drink & quantities
lessons.recycled: Numbers for quantities (Units 1–5). There is/are in the fridge (Unit 4).
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Food, drink & quantities. Like, love, hate, don't mind, prefer."

lessons.title: Present simple — he/she/it: the third person -s rule
lessons.recycled: Present simple I/you/they (Unit 5) now extended to 3rd person. Family members (Unit 3).
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "She likes coffee. He eats pasta every day. She doesn't eat meat. Does he drink wine?"

lessons.title: Talking about food preferences
lessons.recycled: ↻ There is/are (Unit 4), Family (Unit 3), Daily routine (Unit 5), Numbers/quantities.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Role-plays: in a supermarket, at a dinner table."

lessons.title: Spoken course lesson: My favourite meal
lessons.recycled: Units 1–6 active: greetings, to be, nationalities, family, home, routine, present simple.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Free description of favourite food and a typical meal."

lessons.title: Unit 6 exam
lessons.recycled: Units 1–6 full integration. Combining all grammar forms studied.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Listening: identify preferences. Reading: recipe comprehension. Writing: what your family eats."

lessons.title: Jobs, workplaces & study
lessons.recycled: To be (Units 1–2). Numbers for work hours. Routine verbs (Unit 5).
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Jobs: teacher, doctor, etc. Workplaces: hospital, school. Adjectives: busy, interesting."

lessons.title: Can / can't — ability and possibility
lessons.recycled: Languages (Unit 2), Family (Unit 3), Food (Unit 6), Routine (Unit 5).
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "I can speak French. I can't drive. Can you cook? Yes, I can."

lessons.title: Talking about your job and abilities
lessons.recycled: ↻ Greetings (Unit 1), To be (Units 1–2), Present simple (Units 5–6), Nationalities (Unit 2), Family (Unit 3).
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Students describe job, workplace, hours. Role-play: job interview."

lessons.title: Spoken course lesson: What do you do?
lessons.recycled: Units 1–7 fully active. Job interview format recycles greetings, to be, nationalities, present simple, and can.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Free conversation about work and abilities."

lessons.title: Unit 7 exam
lessons.recycled: Units 1–7 integration. CV writing task.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Listening: identify abilities. Reading: job ad. Writing: CV profile. Speaking: mock interview."

lessons.title: Places in a city & transport
lessons.recycled: Hospital/School (Unit 7), Cafe (Unit 6). Numbers for distances.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "City places & Transport. Question words: where, how, how far, how long."

lessons.title: Imperatives & directions + question words review
lessons.recycled: Prepositions of place (Unit 4). Transport vocabulary. Numbers.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Turn left/right, go straight. Positive/negative imperatives. Full question word review."

lessons.title: Getting around the city
lessons.recycled: ↻ There is/are (Unit 4), Routine (Unit 5), Can (Unit 7), Prepositions (Unit 4), Numbers.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Role-plays: asking for directions, buying a ticket."

lessons.title: Spoken course lesson: Getting around
lessons.recycled: Units 1–8 active. Combines home (Unit 4), routine (Unit 5), work (Unit 7).
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Students explain their neighbourhood: what's there, transport used."

lessons.title: Unit 8 exam
lessons.recycled: Units 1–8: directions + city + there is/are + can + routine + transport combined.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Listening: follow directions. Reading: neighbourhood description. Writing: directions from home to work."

lessons.title: Hobbies, sports & expressing opinions
lessons.recycled: Cooking (Unit 6), can (Unit 7) for swimming/running, Sport places (Unit 8).
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Hobbies & Opinions. Linking words: and, but, because."

lessons.title: Present continuous — am/is/are + -ing
lessons.recycled: To be (Units 1–2) as auxiliary. Hobbies vocab. Family (Unit 3). Home (Unit 4).
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "I am reading. Contrast: I read every day vs I am reading now. Spelling: -ing rules."

lessons.title: Talking about hobbies and what you're doing
lessons.recycled: ↻ Present simple for habits (Units 5–6), Family (Unit 3), Routines (Unit 5), City (Unit 8).
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Students describe hobbies, role-play a phone call: What are you doing?"

lessons.title: Spoken course lesson: What do you do for fun?
lessons.recycled: Units 1–9 fully active. The tense contrast lesson.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Free conversation about leisure. Right now vs usually challenge."

lessons.title: Unit 9 exam
lessons.recycled: Units 1–9: both tenses, all domains, family, city, food, work present.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Listening: doing vs hobbies. Reading: blog post. Writing: typical Sunday (both tenses)."

units.title: Past & plans — Revision, future & telling your story

lessons.title: Past time expressions & future plans vocabulary
lessons.recycled: To be (Units 1–2), All vocabulary from all units.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Past & Future time expressions. Was/were intro. Plans: going to, want to. Review all domains."

lessons.title: Was / were (past of to be) + going to (future plans)
lessons.recycled: All to be forms (Units 1–2). Countries (Unit 2). Family (Unit 3). Home (Unit 4).
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "I was at home. Were you at work? I'm going to visit my family. Connect past to present to future."

lessons.title: Telling your story — past, present and future
lessons.recycled: ↻ FULL A1 REVISION. Greetings, to be, possessives, there is/are, present simple, can, imperatives, present continuous, was/were, going to.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Students give a mini-autobiography. Teacher asks questions across all time frames."

lessons.title: Spoken course lesson: My life story
lessons.recycled: Units 1–10 complete revision in free, natural conversation.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "Spoken Course A1 lesson. The student tells their complete story."

lessons.title: A1 Final Exam
lessons.recycled: Complete A1 syllabus — all 10 units, all grammar, all domains, four skills.
lessons.teacher_notes: desc: "LISTENING: life story. READING: profile text. WRITING: 15-sentence profile. SPEAKING: 10-minute free conversation."
